title: Richter transformation with BTK C481S mutation after multiple CLL therapies

patient.chief_complaint: Progressive dyspnea, ascites, and a painful gluteal mass in a patient with CLL
patient.vital_signs: BP 105/65 mmHg, HR 112/min, RR 22/min, Temp 37.7 °C, SpO₂ 92%

1.capabilities: Rapid bedside assessment with point-of-care imaging and routine laboratory panels is available, but invasive sampling or specialized molecular diagnostics are not yet ready.

2.capabilities: The patient can undergo targeted imaging, bone marrow or tissue sampling, and comprehensive flow cytometry while longer-turnaround molecular studies are still pending.

3.capabilities: Full cytogenetic, molecular, and response assessments plus historical treatment data are accessible to guide transplant or cellular therapy planning.

demographics.valid_request_if: Requests for demographics, comorbidities, or social history.
demographics.content:
Age: 77 years
Sex: Male
Performance status: ECOG 1
Comorbidities: CAD with MI 2019 (PTCA/DES), hypertension, AVNRT, COPD GOLD 2, hypothyroidism, severe COVID-19 with VV-ECMO and ICH in 2020
Social: Lives alone, socially isolated, long-term smoker (~40 pack-years), retired circus bear tamer

history_physical.valid_request_if: Requests for presenting symptoms, exam findings, scars, or medications.
history_physical.content: 2025-09-23: Presents with progressive dyspnea, abdominal distension, and painful swelling of the left gluteal region with induration over old scars from animal bites. Left leg edema present. Pulmonary exam: diminished breath sounds with basal crackles. Vital signs as listed. Current meds: beclometasone/formoterol inhaler, HCTZ, levothyroxine, vitamins, pantoprazole, amlodipine, aciclovir, allopurinol, bisoprolol, candesartan, atorvastatin, zanubrutinib 160 mg daily.

baseline_labs.content: Hb 9.2 g/dL, WBC 41 ×10⁹/L (82% lymphocytes), Plt 78 ×10⁹/L. Coagulation: aPTT 31 s, INR 1.1, fibrinogen 281 mg/dL, D-dimer 2987 µg/L FEU. Chemistry: Na 138 mmol/L, K 4.4 mmol/L, creatinine 1.4 mg/dL, AST 185 U/L, ALT 210 U/L, bilirubin 2.8 mg/dL, LDH 852 U/L, uric acid 7.8 mg/dL, albumin 2.8 g/dL, total protein 5.2 g/dL, NT-proBNP 780 pg/mL, Troponin T 0.02 ng/mL, IgG 359 mg/dL.

initial_imaging.valid_request_if: Requests for chest X-ray or ultrasound findings.
initial_imaging.content: Chest X-ray: bilateral pleural effusions with basal atelectasis (right > left); Abdominal ultrasound: four-quadrant ascites, multiple enlarged paraaortic and mesenteric nodes up to 6.5 cm, bulky hepatic hilum conglomerate, hepatomegaly with nodular contour, splenomegaly 18.2 × 9.6 cm. Echocardiogram: LVEF 51% with RV dilatation and pulmonary hypertension.

bone_marrow.content: Hypercellular marrow with replacement by small mature lymphocytes plus a second population of large atypical lymphoid cells. Flow: two B-cell clones – Population 1 CD19⁺/CD20^dim/CD5⁺/CD23⁺/kappa^dim (CLL-like); Population 2 CD19⁺/CD20⁺⁺/CD5⁺/CD22⁺/CD79b⁺/FMC7⁺/kappa-restricted/CD23⁻ consistent with Richter transformation. Plasma cells <1%.

cytogenetics.content: Karyotype: 46,XY,del(11)(q22q23),+12,del(13)(q14q21),del(17)(p13)[17]/46,XY[4]; FISH confirms 17p deletion and trisomy 12.

extended_evaluations.valid_request_if: Requests for CT/PET, biopsy, or thromboembolic work-up.
extended_evaluations.content: CT thorax (2025-09-26): segmental pulmonary embolism right lower lobe with infarction/infarct pneumonia; bilateral pleural effusions. PET-CT (2025-09-30): intensely FDG-avid nodal disease (cervical to pelvic), bulky retroperitoneal/hepatic nodes, avid gluteal mass, uptake in infarct zone. Gluteal biopsy (10-01): diffuse large B-cell lymphoma CD19⁺/CD20⁺/PAX5⁺/CD5⁺/CD23⁻, Ki-67 ~80%. Lumbar puncture: clear CSF, no malignant cells.

molecular_profile.valid_request_if: Requests for NGS, IGHV, BTK mutation, or risk markers.
molecular_profile.content: Sequencing: TP53 mutation VAF 68%, NOTCH1 frameshift VAF 42%, SF3B1 VAF 38%, BTK C481S resistance mutation; IgHV unmutated, ZAP-70 positive.

treatment_history.valid_request_if: Requests for prior CLL therapies and complications.
treatment_history.content: 2005: CLL Binet A observed. 2009: Binet C with autoimmune hemolysis → R-FC ×6 complicated by Pneumocystis pneumonia requiring ventilation. 2013: Relapse → R-bendamustine ×4 (neutropenic fever). 2018: Enrolled in trial, started ibrutinib 420 mg daily (hypertension, GI toxicity, excellent response); stopped during severe COVID-19 in 2020 requiring VV-ECMO with small ICH. 2021: Relapse → rituximab + venetoclax with TLS prophylaxis (partial remission). 2023: Progressive disease with new TP53 mutation → zanubrutinib 320 mg/day (dose reduced to 160 mg for neutropenia and hypertension), partial remission maintained until current Richter transformation.

special_context.valid_request_if: Requests for overall status, supportive needs, or trial eligibility.
special_context.content: Richter transformation with bulky disease, ascites, pleural effusions, pulmonary embolism, and BTK C481S mutation indicating BTK inhibitor resistance. ECOG 1 but significant COPD and prior ECMO injury; lives alone with limited support, hypogammaglobulinemia (IgG 359 mg/dL). Allogeneic SCT not feasible. Requires anticoagulation, infection management, paracentesis, and IVIG replacement.

treatment_plan.action: CT pulmonary angiography to confirm or exclude pulmonary embolism given high clinical suspicion
Ultrasound of gluteal region with biopsy if suspicious for lymphomatous infiltration or abscess
Peripheral blood smear and flow cytometry to characterize lymphocytosis
Reduce or pause antihypertensive medications given hypotension
Staging PET/CT or contrast-enhanced CT for lymphadenopathy assessment

key_findings_quotes: The patient presented to the emergency department with progressive dyspnea and abdominal distension, accompanied by painful swelling of the left gluteal region
CBC: Hb 9.2 g/dL, WBC x 41^9 x10^9/L, Plt 78 x10^9/L
Bilateral pleural effusions with basal atelectasis, more pronounced on the right
multiple enlarged paraaortic and mesenteric lymph nodes up to 6.5 cm, bulky conglomerate at the hepatic hilum
marked RV dilatation with septal flattening (D-sign), consistent with pulmonary hypertension

round_2.working_diagnosis: CLL with Richter transformation to large B-cell lymphoma; pulmonary embolism
differentials: CLL and de novo aggressive lymphoma

treatment_plan.action: Therapeutic anticoagulation for pulmonary embolism with close monitoring of platelet counts
Present at hematology tumor board and evaluate clinical trial options
Organ function assessment prior to chemotherapy (creatinine clearance, hepatitis/HIV serology, ECG, TTE)

key_findings_quotes: Population 2.: CD19+, CD20 ++, CD5+, CD22+, CD79b+, FMC7+ and with kappa light chain restriction, and loss of CD23
A second population of large atypical lymphoid cells was identified, characterized by vesicular chromatin, irregular nuclear contours, prominent nucleoli
Segmental pulmonary embolism in the right lower lobe with wedge-shaped infarction and adjacent infarct pneumonia

Biopsy of gluteal mass: Diffuse infiltration by large atypical B-lymphocytes with vesicular chromatin, prominent nucleoli, and high mitotic activity. CD19+, CD20+, CD79a+, PAX5+, CD5+, CD23--, Ki-67 ~80%
Extensive FDG-avid lymphadenopathy involving cervical, mediastinal, abdominal, and pelvic regions

round_3.working_diagnosis: Chronic lymphocytic leukemia with Richter transformation to diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, Ann Arbor stage IV

treatment_plan.action: Prioritize clinical trial participation, preferably evaluating bispecific antibodies (epcoritamab, glofitamab, mosunetuzumab) or non-covalent BTK inhibitor pirtobrutinib
If trials unavailable, consider R-CHOP-like chemoimmunotherapy with palliative intent, recognizing limited efficacy in high-risk mutations
Therapeutic anticoagulation with apixaban for pulmonary embolism and antibiotic therapy for infarct pneumonia
IVIG replacement for hypogammaglobulinemia; repeated paracentesis and thoracentesis for symptomatic relief
Prednisolone prephase with tumor lysis monitoring if evaluation for trial takes time

key_findings_quotes: TP53 mutation (VAF 68%), NOTCH1 frameshift mutation (VAF 42%), as well as an SF3B1 (VAF 38%). BTK C481S mutation. IgHV unmutated, ZAP-70 positive
Richter transformation (diffuse large B-cell lymphoma type), arising from CLL with high-risk biology

Clinical trial participation should be evaluated, preferably within studies investigating bispecific antibodies (e.g., epcoritamab or glofitamab) in combination with polatuzumab-vedotin or pirtobrutinib
In the absence of suitable trial availability, treatment with an R-CHOP-like regimen in palliative intent is recommended
Supportive measures: Therapeutic anticoagulation with apixaban for pulmonary embolism, antibiotic therapy for infarct pneumonia, and repeated paracentesis and thoracentesis
Allogeneic stem cell transplantation is not feasible given the patient's age and current performance status
